sections.label: Section 2.120
sections.original: οὐκ ἔχω ἐπὶ τίνα
sections.translation: nor know I to whom I should go of all who at the gods ’ altars offer sacrifice .

sections.label: Section 2.122
sections.original: — μόνος δ ’ ἄν , εἰ φῶς τόδ ’ ἦν
sections.translation: Only the son of Phoebus , [*] if he yet saw this light of day —

sections.label: Section 2.125
sections.original: ἦλθεν ἕδρας σκοτίους
sections.translation: Ah ! then then might she have left the dark abode and gates of Hades and have come again , for he would raise the dead to life , till that the thunderbolt ’ s forked flame , hurled by Zeus , smote him .

sections.label: Section 2.130
sections.original: νῦν δὲ τίν ’ ἔτι βίου
sections.translation: But now what further hope of life can I welcome to me ?

sections.label: Section 132
sections.original: — πάντα γὰρ ἤδη τετέλεσται βασιλεῦσι ,
sections.translation: Our lords have ere this done all they could ; on every altar streams the blood of abundant sacrifice ;

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: οὐδ ’ ἔστι κακῶν ἄκος οὐδέν .
sections.translation: yet our sorrows find no cure .

sections.label: Section 136
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἥδ ’ ὀπαδῶν ἐκ δόμων τις ἔρχεται
sections.translation: Lo ! from the house cometh a handmaid weeping ; what shall I be told hath chanced ? Grief may well be pardoned , if aught happeneth to one ’ s master ; yet I fain would learn whether our lady

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: εἴτ ’ οὖν ὄλωλεν εἰδέναι βουλοίμεθ ’ ἄν .
sections.translation: still is living or haply is no more .

sections.label: Section 141
sections.original: καὶ ζῶσαν εἰπεῖν καὶ θανοῦσαν ἔστι σοι .
sections.translation: Alive , yet dead thou may ’ st call her .

sections.label: Section 142
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ἂν αὑτὸς κατθάνοι τε καὶ βλέποι ;
sections.translation: Why , how can the same person be alive , yet dead ?

sections.label: Section 143
sections.original: ἤδη προνωπής ἐστι καὶ ψυχορραγεῖ .
sections.translation: She is sinking even now , and at her last gasp .

sections.label: Section 144
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , οἵας οἷος ὢν ἁμαρτάνεις .
sections.translation: My poor master ! how sad thy lot to lose so good a wife !

sections.label: Section 145
sections.original: οὔπω τόδ ’ οἶδε δεσπότης , πρὶν ἂν πάθῃ .
sections.translation: He did not know his loss , until the blow fell on him .

sections.label: Section 146
sections.original: ἐλπὶς μὲν οὐκέτ ’ ἐστὶ σῴζεσθαι βίον ;
sections.translation: Is there then no more a hope of saving her ?

sections.label: Section 147
sections.original: πεπρωμένη γὰρ ἡμέρα βιάζεται .
sections.translation: None ; the fated day comes on so fast .

sections.label: Section 148
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐπ ’ αὐτῇ πράσσεται τὰ πρόσφορα ;
sections.translation: Are then the fitting rites already taking place o ’ er her body ?

sections.label: Section 149
sections.original: κόσμος γ ’ ἕτοιμος , ᾧ σφε συνθάψει πόσις .
sections.translation: Death ’ s garniture is ready , wherewith her lord will bury her .

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: ἴστω νυν εὐκλεής γε κατθανουμένη
sections.translation: Well let her know , though die she must , her fame ranks far above any other wife ’ s beneath the sun .

sections.label: Section 152
sections.original: πῶς δ ’ οὐκ ἀρίστη ; τίς δ ’ ἐναντιώσεται ;
sections.translation: Far above ! of course it does ; who will gainsay it ? What must the woman be who hath surpassed her ? For how could any wife have shown a clearer regard

sections.label: Section 155
sections.original: πόσιν προτιμῶσ ’ ἢ θέλουσ ’ ὑπερθανεῖν ;
sections.translation: for her lord than by offering in his stead to die ? Thus much the whole city knows right well ; but thou shalt hear with wonder what she did within the house . For when she knew the fatal day was come , she washed her fair white skin with water from the stream ,

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: ἐλούσατʼ , ἐκ δ ’ ἑλοῦσα κεδρίνων δόμων
sections.translation: then from her cedar chests drew forth vesture and ornaments and robed herself becomingly ; next , standing before the altar-hearth , she prayed , Mistress mine , behold ! I pass beneath the earth ; to thee in suppliant wise will I my latest prayer address ;

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: τέκν ’ ὀρφανεῦσαι τἀμά · καὶ τῷ μὲν φίλην
sections.translation: be mother to my orphans , and to my boy unite a loving bride , to my daughter a noble husband . Let them not die , as I , their mother , perish now , untimely in their youth , but let them live their glad lives out , happy in their native land .

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: πάντας δὲ βωμούς , οἳ κατ ’ Ἀδμήτου δόμους ,
sections.translation: To every altar in Admetus ’ halls she went and crowned them and prayed , plucking from myrtle boughs their foliage , with never a tear or groan , nor did her coming trouble change the colour of her comely face .

sections.label: Section 175
sections.original: κἄπειτα θάλαμον ἐσπεσοῦσα καὶ λέχος ,
sections.translation: Anon into her bridal bower she burst , and then her tears brake forth and thus she cried , O couch , whereon I loosed my maiden state for the man in whose cause I die , farewell ! no hate I feel for thee ; for me alone hast thou undone ,

sections.label: Section 180
sections.original: μόνην · προδοῦναι γάρ σ ’ ὀκνοῦσα καὶ πόσιν
sections.translation: dying as I die from fear of betraying thee and my lord . Some other wife will make thee hers , more blest maybe than me , but not more chaste . And she fell upon her knees and kissed it , till with her gushing tears the whole bed was wet .

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δὲ πολλῶν δακρύων εἶχεν κόρον ,
sections.translation: At last , when she had had her fill of weeping , she tore herself from the bed and hurried headlong forth , and oft as she was leaving the chamber turned she back and cast herself once more upon the couch ; while her children were weeping as they clung to their mother ’ s robes ;

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: ἔκλαιον · ἣ δὲ λαμβάνουσ ’ ἐς ἀγκάλας
sections.translation: but she took them each in turn in her arms and kissed them fondly , as a dying mother might . And all the servants in the house fell a-crying in sorrow for their mistress ; but she held out her hand to each , nor was there one so mean

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: ὃν οὐ προσεῖπε καὶ προσερρήθη πάλιν .
sections.translation: but she gave him a word and took his answer back . Such are the sorrows in the halls of Admetus . Dying he had died once for all , but by avoiding death he hath a legacy of grief that he will ne ’ er forget .

sections.label: Section 199
sections.original: ἦ που στενάζει τοισίδ ’ Ἄδμητος κακοῖς ,
sections.translation: Doubtless Admetus sorrows in this calamity ,

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: ἐσθλῆς γυναικὸς εἰ στερηθῆναί σφε χρή ;
sections.translation: if he must lose so good a wife .

sections.label: Section 201
sections.original: κλαίει γ ’ ἄκοιτιν ἐν χεροῖν φίλην ἔχων ,
sections.translation: Ah yes ! he weeps , holding in his arms his darling wife , and prays her not to leave him , impossible request ! for she is worn and wasted with illness , and lies [*] exhausted , a sad burden in his arms .

sections.label: Section 205
sections.original: ὅμως δὲ καίπερ σμικρὸν ἐμπνέουσ ’ ἔτι
sections.translation: Still , though her breath comes short and scant , she yearns to gaze yet on the sunshine , for nevermore , but now the last and latest time her eye shall see his radiant orb . [*] But I will go , thy presence to announce ,

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: οὐ γάρ τι πάντες εὖ φρονοῦσι κοιράνοις ,
sections.translation: for ’ tis not all who have the goodwill to stand by their masters with kindly hearts in adversity . But thou of old hast been my master ’ s friend .

sections.label: Section 1.213
sections.original: — ἰὼ Ζεῦ , τίς ἂν πῶς πᾷ πόρος κακῶν
sections.translation: O Zeus , what way out of these sorrows can be found ? how can we loose the bonds of fate that bind our lord ?

sections.label: Section 1.215
sections.original: — αἰαῖ · εἶσί τις ; ἢ τέμω τρίχα ,
sections.translation:
Comes some one forth ? Am I at once to cut my hair , and cast the sable robe about me ?
Too plainly , ay too plainly , friends ; still let us to heaven pray ; for the gods ’ power is very great .

sections.label: Section 1.220
sections.original: — ὦναξ Παιάν ,
sections.translation:
O king Paean , devise for Admetus some means of escape from his sorrows .
Yes , yes , contrive it ; for thou in days gone by didst find salvation for him , so now be thou a saviour from the toils of death

sections.label: Section 1.225
sections.original: φόνιον δ ’ ἀπόπαυσον Ἅιδαν .
sections.translation: and stay bloodthirsty Hades .

sections.label: Section 1.226
sections.original: — παπαῖ ⋯
sections.translation:
Woe ! woe ! alas ! Thou son of Pheres , woe ! Ah , thy fate in losing thy wife !
Is not this enough to make thee slay thyself , ah ! more than cause enough to tie the noose aloft and fit it to the neck ?

sections.label: Section 1.230
sections.original: οὐρανίῳ πελάσσαι ;
sections.translation:
Yea , for to-day wilt thou witness the death of her that was not merely dear , but dearest of the dear .
Look , look ! she cometh even now , her husband with her , from the house .
Cry aloud and wail , O land of Pherae ,

sections.label: Section 1.235
sections.original: — βόασον ὦ , στέναξον , ὦ Φεραία
sections.translation: wail for the best of women , as with sickness worn she passes ’ neath the earth to Hades , lord below .

sections.label: Section 238
sections.original: οὔποτε φήσω γάμον εὐφραίνειν
sections.translation: Never , never will I say that marriage brings more joy than grief , as I

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: τεκμαιρόμενος καὶ τάσδε τύχας
sections.translation: conjecture by the past and witness these misfortunes of our king , for he when widowed of this noble wife will for the future lead a life that is no life at all .

sections.label: Section 1.244
sections.original: Ἅλιε καὶ φάος ἁμέρας ,
sections.translation: O sun-god , lamp of day !

sections.label: Section 1.245
sections.original: οὐράνιαί τε δῖναι νεφέλας δρομαίου .
sections.translation: O scudding clouds that dance along the sky !

sections.label: Section 1.246
sections.original: ὁρᾷ σὲ κἀμέ , δύο κακῶς πεπραγότας ,
sections.translation: He sees us both with anguish bowed , albeit guiltless of any crime against the gods , for the which thy death is due .

sections.label: Section 1.248
sections.original: γαῖά τε καὶ μελάθρων στέγαι
sections.translation: O earth , O sheltering roof , and ye my maiden chambers in my native land Iolcos !

sections.label: Section 1.250
sections.original: ἔπαιρε σαυτήν , ὦ τάλαινα , μὴ προδῷς ·
sections.translation: Lift thyself , unhappy wife , forsake me not ; entreat the mighty gods to pity us .

sections.label: Section 2.252
sections.original: ὁρῶ δίκωπον ὁρῶ σκάφος ἐν λίμνᾳ ·
sections.translation: I see the two-oared skiff , I see it ; and Charon , death ’ s ferryman , his hand upon the boatman ’ s pole ,

sections.label: Section 2.255
sections.original: μ ’ ἤδη καλεῖ · Τί μέλλεις ;
sections.translation: is calling me e ’ en now , Why lingerest thou ? Hasten . Thou art keeping me . Thus in his eager haste he hurries me .

sections.label: Section 2.257
sections.original: οἴμοι , πικράν γε τήνδε μοι ναυκληρίαν
sections.translation: Ah me ! bitter to me is this voyage thou speakest of . Unhappy wife , what woes are ours !

sections.label: Section 2.259
sections.original: ἄγει μ ’ ἄγει τις · ἄγει μέ τις — οὐχ ὁρᾷς ;—
sections.translation: One draws me , draws me hence , seest thou not ?

sections.label: Section 2.260
sections.original: νεκύων ἐς αὐλάν ,
sections.translation: to the courts of death , winged Hades glaring from beneath his dark brows . What wilt thou with me ? Unhand me . On what a journey am I setting out , most wretched woman I !

sections.label: Section 2.264
sections.original: οἰκτρὰν φίλοισιν , ἐκ δὲ τῶν μάλιστ ’ ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: Bitter journey to thy friends , yet most of all to me

sections.label: Section 2.265
sections.original: καὶ παισίν , οἷς δὴ πένθος ἐν κοινῷ τόδε .
sections.translation: and to thy babes , the partners in this sorrow .

sections.label: Section 266
sections.original: μέθετε μέθετέ μ ’ ἤδη .
sections.translation:
Hands off ! hands off at once !
Lay me down , I cannot stand . Hades standeth near ; and with its gloom steals night upon my eyes .

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: τέκνα , τέκνʼ , οὐκέτι δὴ
sections.translation: O my children , my children , ye have no mother now . Fare ye well , my babes , live on beneath the light !

sections.label: Section 273
sections.original: οἴμοι · τόδ ’ ἔπος λυπρὸν ἀκούω
sections.translation: Woe is me ! this is a message of sorrow to me , worse than aught that death can do .

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: μὴ πρός σε θεῶν τλῇς με προδοῦναι ,
sections.translation: Steel not thy heart to leave me , I implore , by heaven , by thy babes whom thou wilt make orphans ; nay , raise thyself , have courage . For if thou die I can no longer live ; my life , my death are in thy hands ; thy love is what I worship .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: Ἄδμηθʼ , ὁρᾷς γὰρ τἀμὰ πράγμαθ ’ ὡς ἔχει ,
sections.translation: Admetus , lo ! thou seest how it is with me ; to thee I fain would tell my wishes ere I die . Thee I set before myself , and instead of living have ensured thy life , and so I die , though I need not have died for thee ,

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἄνδρα τε σχεῖν Θεσσαλῶν ὃν ἤθελον ,
sections.translation: but might have taken for my husband whom I would of the Thessalians , and have had a home blest with royal power ; reft of thee , with my children orphans , I cared not to live , nor , though crowned with youth ’ s fair gifts , wherein I used to joy , did I grudge them .
